Title: Evolution of an atypical de-epoxidase for photoprotection in the green lineage

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: npq1 mutant in 137c background; Arabidopsis vde1 mutant
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Atypical violaxanthin de-epoxidase; related to bacterial lycopene cyclase/CruA-CruP-like family
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes de-epoxidation of violaxanthin to zeaxanthin in the xanthophyll cycle under excess light
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Violaxanthin-to-zeaxanthin branch of the xanthophyll cycle
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Terminal/committed de-epoxidation step for zeaxanthin formation in the cycle
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Photoprotection; regulation of light harvesting; protection from oxidative stress; adaptation to changing light conditions
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes zeaxanthin accumulation under high light and is required for normal non-photochemical quenching/photoprotection
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CVDE was identified as the gene mutated in the Chlamydomonas npq1 mutant. 2. Genomic complementation restored high-light-induced zeaxanthin accumulation, demonstrating that CVDE is required for xanthophyll-cycle de-epoxidation. 3. Chlamydomonas CVDE encodes an atypical VDE unrelated to plant VDE and localized on the stromal side of the thylakoid membrane. 4. Codon-optimized CrCVDE targeted to Arabidopsis chloroplasts complemented the Arabidopsis vde1 mutant, supporting conserved de-epoxidase function across species.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Protein is not homologous to plant-type VDE; phylogenetic analysis suggests origin from an ancient de-epoxidase in the green lineage. The paper emphasizes evolutionary and localization divergence in achieving the same zeaxanthin-producing function.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key enzyme responsible for zeaxanthin synthesis from violaxanthin under excess light
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genetic mapping of npq1; mutation identification by PCR/sequencing; genomic complementation in Chlamydomonas; heterologous complementation of Arabidopsis vde1; HPLC carotenoid profiling after high light; NPQ measurements; immunoblotting; thylakoid fractionation and protease protection assay
